name: Yoshitsune
description: Minamoto no Yoshitsune was a military commander of the Minamoto clan of Japan during the late Heian and early Kamakura periods. Born Ushiwakamaru, he was the ninth son of Minamoto no Yoshitomo. After his father's death, he was sent to Kurama Temple and later to Hiraizumi, Mutsu Province, where he was sheltered by Fujiwara no Hidehira. He became a key figure in the Genpei War, leading the Minamoto clan to victory against the Taira clan. His brilliant military strategies and tactical genius were instrumental in battles such as Ichi-no-Tani and Dan-no-ura. Despite his successes, his elder brother Minamoto no Yoritomo, the founder and first shogun of the Kamakura shogunate, grew suspicious of Yoshitsune's popularity and power. Yoritomo eventually turned against him, leading to Yoshitsune's exile and eventual death. Yoshitsune is celebrated as a tragic hero, a symbol of loyalty, bravery, and military prowess. His life has been romanticized in many Japanese literary works, including the Tale of the Heike, and he remains a popular figure in Japanese folklore and popular culture, often portrayed as a charismatic and skilled warrior betrayed by his own brother.
subtitle: Tragic hero of the Genpei War

keyMyths.title: Yoshitsune and Benkei on Gojo Bridge
keyMyths.description: Yoshitsune, disguised, defeats the warrior monk Benkei on Gojo Bridge, leading to Benkei becoming his loyal retainer. This demonstrates Yoshitsune's martial prowess and ability to attract strong allies.
keyMyths.source: Various folk tales and historical accounts

keyMyths.title: The Flight to Hiraizumi
keyMyths.description: Yoshitsune, pursued by his brother Yoritomo, flees to Hiraizumi seeking refuge with Fujiwara no Hidehira. This showcases his vulnerability and the betrayal he faced despite his accomplishments.

keyMyths.title: Yoshitsune's Death
keyMyths.description: Betrayed and surrounded, Yoshitsune commits suicide (seppuku) rather than be captured by Yoritomo's forces. This act solidifies his status as a tragic hero, choosing honor over surrender.

sources.author: Unknown
sources.description: A classic Japanese epic that recounts the Genpei War and prominently features Yoshitsune.
sources.text: A classic Japanese epic that recounts the Genpei War and prominently features Yoshitsune.
